retention_rules: Keep durable LDD/Tianma rules in active memory.
Keep only the latest valid LDD checkpoint in active memory.
Move old 2026-05 account snapshot memories into historical_snapshot or superseded_memory after newer records exist.
Archive detailed historical snapshots into records/ldd and reports/ldd before recommending active-memory cleanup.
Never silently delete durable project context.
active_memory_rules: Active memory should store durable rules, current checkpoint, open risks, and latest valid command context.
Detailed account numbers should live in runtime records and reports once archived.
Superseded snapshots should be removable from active memory only after user review.
